author: opus
text:
compat: forward-compat catch-all on Link and ArchivedChild

bl-2148 made state-branch commits propagate across machines, so any
field added by a newer bl is at risk of being silently dropped on
the next save by an older client. Task and Note already had a
flatten extra; extend the same safety net to the two state-branch
sibling types that can grow new fields.

Link: extra carries unknown sibling keys; PartialEq/Eq compares
type+target only so link_add/link_rm Vec::contains stays stable when
an older client edits a task whose links carry forward-compat
fields. ArchivedChild: same exposure (delivery sha, status snapshot,
etc.); extracted to its own module to keep task.rs under the
300-line cap.

Tests cover nested unknown fields round-tripping through save/load
for both types, plus an end-to-end version-mismatch where the older
binary loads a future-shaped task file, mutates an unrelated field,
saves, and reloads with every extra surviving.
